title: Navitas (NVTS) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Navitas Semiconductor reported Q1 2026 financial results on May 5, 2026, with earnings call at 5 p.m. ET. Key participants included Chris Allexandre (President and CEO) and Tonya Stevens (CFO). Navitas reported Q1 2026 revenue of $8.6 million, representing 18% sequential growth from the prior quarter's $7.3 million. The company's high-power markets grew 35% year-over-year and now constitute the majority of total revenue as management executes its strategic pivot away from mobile and consumer segments. Gross margin improved by 30 basis points to 39.0% from 38.7%, driven by favorable product mix. Operating expenses remained essentially flat at $15.0 million, while the operating loss narrowed to $11.7 million from $12.1 million sequentially. Net loss per share improved to $0.04 from $0.05. The balance sheet strengthened with $221 million in cash and zero debt, providing substantial runway for growth investments. The company concentrates exclusively on four high-growth segments: AI data centers, grid infrastructure, performance computing, and industrial electrification. Management emphasized that having both GaN and SiC technologies provides key differentiation in customer engagements, particularly within AI infrastructure applications. For Q2 2026, management projects revenue of $10.0 million (±$0.5 million), representing over 16% sequential growth. Non-GAAP gross margin is expected at 39.25% (±75 basis points), with operating expenses remaining flat between $14.5-$15.5 million.
